items.text: The Company has been affected by an industry-wide undersupply of component parts and anticipates the shortages may continue to affect deliveries into 2023.
items.rationale: Extends the existing component shortage risk by noting continued impact into 2023.

items.text: The likelihood or severity of these unexpected events may increase due to the effects of climate change.
items.rationale: Introduces climate change as a factor increasing likelihood/severity of unexpected events, a new specific risk driver.

items.text: Transition Risks Related to Climate Change.
items.rationale: Section heading with no substantive content.

items.text: The Company has ongoing product development programs intended to address changing customer demand in the context of climate change and achieve its targeted reductions in emissions.
items.rationale: Describes company mitigation programs related to already-existing climate/emissions risk.

items.text: These involve the continuing development of compliant clean diesel powertrains and the design, manufacture, and sale of alternative powertrain commercial vehicles (e.g., battery-electric, hybrid, hydrogen fuel cell, and hydrogen combustion).
items.rationale: Provides specific detail on powertrain technologies as part of existing emissions risk discussion.

items.text: If the Company is not successful in addressing the risks noted above, there may be a material adverse impact on its business, operations, and financial condition.
items.rationale: Generic cautionary statement about potential adverse impact if risks aren't addressed.

items.text: Some of the Company’s products include telematics which provide over-the-air software updates, advanced fleet management tools and real-time data analytics on driver and vehicle performance.
items.rationale: Introduces new product feature (telematics with over-the-air updates) creating new cybersecurity/data exposure.

items.text: The Company has not experienced any notable security incidents that would have a material impact on the results of operations and financial condition of the Company.
items.rationale: Reassurance statement that no material security incidents occurred, not a new risk.

items.text: Certain dealers and suppliers have reported they have experienced cyberattacks and those have not caused any material impact to the Company.
items.rationale: Discloses actual cyberattacks experienced by dealers/suppliers, a concrete new risk event.

items.text: In accordance with international sanctions, in February 2022 the Company suspended truck and parts sales to Russia and Belarus.
items.rationale: Introduces new geopolitical risk from sanctions and suspension of sales to Russia/Belarus.

items.text: The Company has no factories in Russia and has managed export sales to Russia through independent dealers.
items.rationale: Provides context on exposure to Russia within the new geopolitical risk discussion.

items.text: In 2021, 2,500 trucks were sold into Russia and Belarus.
items.rationale: Quantifies prior sales exposure to Russia/Belarus, supporting the new geopolitical risk.

items.text: The Company also sold parts in these markets through a third-party owned warehouse.
items.rationale: Additional detail on parts sales structure in Russia/Belarus market.

items.text: The trucks were sold on a fully paid-up basis; accordingly, the Company does not have significant receivables exposure.
items.rationale: Clarifies financial exposure (receivables) related to the new Russia risk.

items.text: Inventory balances are not significant.
items.rationale: Further clarifies limited financial exposure (inventory) tied to Russia risk.

items.text: The conflict may affect energy supplies in Europe.
items.rationale: Introduces new risk of energy supply disruption in Europe due to conflict.

items.text: If the availability of energy in Europe is severely constrained, truck delivery volumes could be impacted.
items.rationale: Specifies potential operational impact (delivery volumes) from energy constraints, a new concrete risk.

items.text: The Company continues to monitor the situation closely.
items.rationale: Generic statement about monitoring the situation, no new risk.

items.text: The conflict has not had a significant impact on the results of operations or cash flows of the Company.
items.rationale: Reassurance that conflict has not yet impacted results, not a new risk.

items.text: The potential future impact on the Company’s business will depend on further developments, including the severity and duration of the conflict and its effect on European and global economic conditions.
items.rationale: Generic forward-looking statement about uncertainty, no specific new risk detail.

items.text: The effects of the COVID-19 pandemic decreased over the course of 2022 but still caused disruptions to the global supply chain, in particular the undersupply of component parts and semiconductor chips.
items.rationale: Updates existing COVID-related supply chain risk with 2022 status.

items.text: Emissions Requirements and Reduction Targets.
items.rationale: Section heading with no substantive content.

items.text: PACCAR’s operations and products are subject to extensive statutory and regulatory requirements governing greenhouse gas and non-greenhouse gas emissions.
items.rationale: Reiterates existing regulatory exposure to emissions standards.

items.text: These include standards imposed by the U.S.
items.rationale: Fragment introducing a list, no substantive new content.

items.text: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the European Union, U.S. state regulatory agencies (such as the California Air Resources Board), regulatory agencies in other international markets where the Company operates, and non-binding international accords related to climate change.
items.rationale: Specifies regulatory bodies involved in existing emissions compliance risk.

items.text: The primary laws and regulations are the EPA’s Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards and Fuel Efficiency Standards for Medium and Heavy-Duty Engines and Vehicles, the Regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council on the Monitoring and Reporting of CO2 Emissions from Fuel Consumption of New Heavy-Duty Vehicles, and the Heavy-Duty Omnibus Regulation of the California Air Resources Board.
items.rationale: Names specific existing regulations, adding detail to known compliance risk.

items.text: PACCAR established its science-based greenhouse gas emission reduction targets to meet the goals of the Paris Agreement.
items.rationale: Describes company's voluntary emission targets, expanding on existing climate risk narrative.

items.text: The Company continually monitors developments in emissions and climate change-related laws and regulations in the markets in which the Company conducts business.
items.rationale: Generic statement about monitoring regulatory developments.

items.text: The Company’s product planning through 2030 is aligned with these statutory and regulatory requirements, and uses a climate change scenario analysis to limit global warming to below 2°C.
items.rationale: Details product planning alignment with climate goals, expanding existing risk discussion.

items.text: Even without legislation to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, PACCAR expects to continue to significantly invest in technologies to improve fuel efficiency for its customers, which would also reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
items.rationale: Generic reassurance about continued investment in efficiency technology.

items.text: PACCAR expects that climate change-related laws, regulations, and international accords will continue to evolve.
items.rationale: Generic forward-looking statement about regulatory evolution.

items.text: The Company cannot reasonably predict whether future laws, regulations, and international accords could materially increase its environmental compliance costs, alter its product development strategy, or impact its business, financial condition, or results of operations.
items.rationale: Standard cautionary language about inability to predict regulatory impact.

items.text: The Company’s telematics depend on cellular frequency allocations regulated by government agencies and collected data is subject to various privacy laws and government regulations.
items.rationale: Introduces new regulatory/privacy risk tied to telematics data and cellular frequency allocation.
